title: Salted Honey & Yoghurt Cheesecake
summary: This is a lighter version of the cheesecake you might already be familiar with: it’s rich thanks to a combo of soft ricotta, tangy yoghurt and honey.

method.description:
Line a sieve with a clean tea towel and set it over a bowl. Add
the yoghurt and ricotta to the lined sieve, then leave to strain for a
couple of hours at room temperature.
Preheat the oven to 180°C and line a 15cm springform tin with two squares of baking paper.

Tip the ricotta and yoghurt into a large bowl, discarding any of
the liquid that has come out of it, and whisk until smooth.

Add all but a tablespoon of the honey and whisk again. Add the
eggs one at a time, whisking until you have a totally smooth batter
before adding the next one. Add the vanilla extract, the zest of the
orange and the sea salt, then whisk until just combined.

Pour the cheesecake mixture into the lined tin and bake in the
middle of the oven for 45 minutes. When it is done, it should be set
around the edges but jiggle in the middle.

Leave to cool to room temperature, then pop your cheesecake
into the fridge to chill for a couple of hours.

When ready to serve, finely chop the pistachios. Remove the
cheesecake from the tin and top with the remaining honey and
chopped pistachios.

chefs.name: Ben Lippett
chefs.summary: Ben Lippett (@dinnerbyben) is a cook, food writer, recipe developer, and co-founder of Dr Sting's Hot Honey. Ben spent years working at a spate of restaurants in the UK and Australia – including a brief but rewarding stint at a butcher's – before making the leap to food media.
